use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::collections::HashMap;

// GOOD: interior mutability fields are private, with public methods
pub struct SharedState {
    cache: Mutex<HashMap<String, String>>,
    counters: Mutex<Vec<u64>>,
    flags: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32,
}

impl SharedState {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: Mutex::new(HashMap::new()),
            counters: Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
            flags: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0),
        }
    }

    pub fn get_cached(&self, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
        self.cache.lock().unwrap().get(key).cloned()
    }

    pub fn set_cached(&self, key: String, value: String) {
        self.cache.lock().unwrap().insert(key, value);
    }

    pub fn add_counter(&self, value: u64) {
        self.counters.lock().unwrap().push(value);
    }

    pub fn set_flag(&self, flag: u32) {
        self.flags.fetch_or(flag, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
    }
}
